baseline.source: Spec 01 viability report - 5 workflow runs from 2026-03-25 to 2026-03-26

optimizations_applied.name: CocoaPods Caching
optimizations_applied.description: Cache ios/Pods directory with actions/cache@v4, 55% faster pod install

optimizations_applied.name: pod install --silent
optimizations_applied.description: 84% reduction in pod install output (log readability improvement, no time savings)

optimizations_applied.name: UDID Targeting
optimizations_applied.description: More precise simulator selection (log readability improvement, no time savings)

improvements.additional_savings_note: Actual savings significantly exceed optimizations implemented, likely due to GitHub Actions runner performance variability, network conditions, and baseline measurement variance

success_metrics.log_target_note: 50% target was aspirational; 187-line xcodebuild destination warning is unavoidable with current tooling. Achieved maximum possible reduction through pod install --silent (84% pod output reduction).

initial_runs.note: Initial cache population after implementing caching

notes.performance_variability: GitHub Actions runner performance shows natural variability. Baseline avg of 13.82 min had a range of 9.77-19.47 min. Optimized avg of 9.84 min has a range of 7.52-11.88 min, showing improved consistency.
notes.optimization_contributions: While specific optimizations account for ~27 seconds of time savings (CocoaPods caching + Metro wait reduction), the total improvement of ~239 seconds includes additional factors such as runner variability, network conditions, and baseline measurement.
notes.cache_reliability: 100% cache hit rate demonstrates excellent cache stability and reliability. All validation runs successfully restored the cached Pods directory.
notes.test_reliability: 100% test pass rate across all validation runs confirms that optimizations introduced zero quality regressions.
notes.log_verbosity: xcodebuild destination warning (187 lines) remains unavoidable but has zero performance impact. All suppression methods were exhaustively tested and documented.
